angle: Version numbering drift: ENV_REFERENCE.md uses multiple version-label columns ('graduated', 'current', 'spec 017', 'PI-A2', etc.) but lacks any mapping to the v3.x.x.x release tags. Cross-check whether the flags claimed as 'graduated' in v3.7.0.0 changelog actually have their defaults flipped in search-flags.ts.

title: v3.7 says retrieval-shape routing is on, runtime keeps it opt-in
evidence: v3.7 says "The five-class classifier is on for everyone" and "a simple single-hop lookup skips the graph-relation expansion"; `search-flags.ts:437-444` says "Default: FALSE (opt-in)" and returns `isOptInEnabled('SPECKIT_RETRIEVAL_CLASS_ROUTING')`; `ENV_REFERENCE.md:294` also documents default `false`.
fix: Revise the v3.7 changelog to distinguish the always-on classifier from the default-off SingleHop graph/degree suppression, or flip the runtime default only if the cut verdict is superseded by a measured promotion.

title: 028 graduated flag table still names removed `_V1` env vars
evidence: The table lists `SPECKIT_LEXICAL_GROUNDING_V1` as ON, but `search-flags.ts:682` reads `process.env.SPECKIT_LEXICAL_GROUNDING`; the same unsuffixed pattern appears for noise floor, cite-with-caveat, evidence-gap, and envelope fidelity at `search-flags.ts:703`, `:726`, `:745`, and `:763`.
fix: Update the 028 feature-flags table to the unsuffixed env names and add a short `formerly *_V1` note or link to the cleanup mapping.

title: ENV reference has an `added in version` column without release-tag values
evidence: The table header says `added in version`, but rows use labels like `graduated`, `current`, `PI-A2`, and `spec 017` (`ENV_REFERENCE.md:33-45`), while v3.7 claims a concrete release event: "twelve flags earned their way to default-on or enforcing" (`changelog/v3.7.0.0.md:158`).
fix: Add a release-history/mapping column or appendix mapping labels such as `graduated` and 028/005 flags to concrete release tags like `v3.7.0.0`.

title: ENV quick table mixes `v1` display label with unsuffixed governing env var
evidence: The row label is `Lexical grounding v1` but the governing env var is `SPECKIT_LEXICAL_GROUNDING`; packet cleanup says the canonical mapping is `SPECKIT_LEXICAL_GROUNDING_V1` to `SPECKIT_LEXICAL_GROUNDING` (`007-dark-flag-graduation/009-flag-name-cleanup/implementation-summary.md:54`).
fix: Rename the display label to `Lexical grounding` and add an explicit legacy-name mapping note for the `_V1` cleanup.
